frameworks.name: sensory adaptation
frameworks.one_line: prolonged exposure shifts psychometric function toward the adaptor

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / decision-making
frameworks.one_line: adaptation also alters how sensory evidence is accumulated for each choice

frameworks.name: normative response to context change
frameworks.one_line: decision changes reflect updated implicit expectations about upcoming stimuli

core_question: Do changes in the decision-making process contribute to perceptual adaptation alongside sensory shifts?
key_assumptions: adaptation can be decomposed into sensory and decision components
response times index decision-making changes beyond psychometric shifts
implicit expectations about stimuli shape choice dynamics
manipulations: prolonged exposure to happy versus sad facial expressions
measurement of both psychometric function and response times
key_findings: exposure to an emotional expression shifts the psychometric function toward the adaptor
response times decline overall after adaptation
response times show asymmetry, with faster responses opposite the adapting category
interpretations: adaptation changes how much sensory evidence is accumulated for each choice
adaptation modifies implicit expectations as a normative response to context change
